info.name: CST391 Milestone 4 — API evidence (screenshots)
info.description:
Use this collection for Milestone 4 **Testing and Evidence** (Postman screenshots).

**Before you start**
1. Run `npm run dev` (local) or set `baseUrl` to your Vercel app.
2. Apply `Docs/Milestone Guides/CST-391-Milestone4/playlists_schema.sql` to the same Postgres your app uses.
3. Ensure `albums` / `tracks` data exists so `trackId` (e.g. `1`) is valid.

**Screenshot rule (assignment)**
For **each** request below, capture a successful response and **label** the image (or filename) with:
- the **HTTP method and path** (e.g. `POST /api/playlists`)
- the **user type**: **customer** or **admin**

Use the **Screenshot label** line in each request’s description as the exact text to copy.

**Suggested order**
1. Run all **Customer** requests top to bottom (copy `id` from create → `playlistId`).
2. Run **Admin** requests (uses same `playlistId` for delete demo, or any id from GET admin).

request.description:
**Screenshot label:** `POST /api/playlists` — **customer**

201 Created. Copy `id` from the response into collection variable `playlistId` (Postman: … menu → Edit → Variables, or set manually).

**Screenshot label:** `GET /api/playlists?ownerUserId={uuid}` — **customer**

200 OK — lists playlists for that owner.

**Screenshot label:** `GET /api/playlists` — **customer**

200 OK — lists all playlists (open listing for testing). If your instructor only wants one GET screenshot, use either this or the `ownerUserId` variant.

**Screenshot label:** `POST /api/playlists/{id}/tracks` — **customer**

Requires `playlistId` set. `trackId` must exist in table `tracks`.

**Screenshot label:** `DELETE /api/playlists/{id}/tracks/{trackId}` — **customer**

204 No Content on success. If you already deleted the track, you may get 404 — re-run POST add track first.

**Screenshot label:** `DELETE /api/admin/playlists/{id}` — **admin**

204 No Content. Use a `playlistId` you are done testing with (creates a new playlist with POST if needed).
